pattern: Deleting each occurrence of the target digit yields a candidate string of equal length, so comparing those same-length numeric strings directly picks the largest result.
hints: Every deletion leaves a string of the same length, so a plain string comparison already orders the candidates by numeric value.
Generate the string formed by removing each occurrence of the digit and keep the largest.
A greedy shortcut: removing an occurrence where the next character is strictly larger increases the value, so prefer such a spot, otherwise drop the last occurrence.

edge_inputs.description: two occurrences, removing the earlier one is better
both removals give the same string
removing earlier occurrence wins because the next digit is larger
occurrence followed by smaller digit means drop the later one

stress.description: long digit strings with several occurrences of the target
stress.note: guarantees the digit appears at least once
